questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: The 'Evergreen Revolution' proposed by M.S. Swaminathan differs from the initial 'Green Revolution' primarily by emphasizing:

options.text: The use of chemical fertilizers and high-yielding variety (HYV) seeds to maximize short-term output.
Increasing food production through the adoption of dwarfing genes in cereal crops.
Achieving lasting productivity increases without causing ecological harm.
The transition from traditional rice varieties to high-yielding Indica varieties.

questions.explanation: While the Green Revolution focused on yield through modern techniques and dwarfing genes, the Evergreen Revolution is a pro-nature, pro-poor concept aimed at productivity without ecological harm, linking livelihood security with ecological security.
questions.takeaway: The Evergreen Revolution is the ecological evolution of the Green Revolution, focusing on sustainability.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Swaminathan proposed the “Evergreen Revolution” for lasting productivity without ecological harm. Evergreen revolution is pro-nature and pro-poor, linking livelihood security with ecological security.
